adds.rationale: Symbotic is the cleanest US-listed pure-play on warehouse robotics with a Walmart anchor partnership and an accelerating revenue ramp ratified by its Feb-2024 quarter; a more thesis-aligned robotics expression than the BOTZ ETF it replaces.

news_evidence.summary: Symbotic reports F1Q24 revenue $364M up 79% YoY, backlog $23B, beats consensus; shares jump on accelerating Walmart deployment.
Walmart expands Symbotic deployment commitment to all 42 regional distribution centers; revenue visibility extends through 2030.
Symbotic guides F2Q24 revenue $400-420M and reiterates full-year >$1.6B; analysts upgrade citing capacity ramp at three new Walmart sites.

adds.rationale: Vistra owns the Comanche Peak nuclear plant plus a large merchant gas/coal fleet sitting next to Texas data-center demand growth; the September 2023 closing of the Energy Harbor acquisition added Davis-Besse/Perry/Beaver Valley nuclear, making VST the largest competitive nuclear operator after Constellation and a direct beneficiary of the AI-power thesis the profile names.

news_evidence.summary: Vistra closes $3.4B Energy Harbor acquisition, adding ~4 GW of nuclear capacity (Davis-Besse, Perry, Beaver Valley) and ~1M retail customers; deal announced March 2023, closed March 2024.
ERCOT forecasts Texas peak demand to grow ~40% by 2030 driven by data centers, hydrogen, and industrial electrification; merchant generators with Texas exposure flagged as primary beneficiaries.
Vistra Q4-2023 results: management raises 2024 EBITDA guidance citing capacity tightness, retail margin expansion, and contribution from newly-acquired nuclear fleet.

removes.rationale: Replacing the broad robotics ETF (heavy in legacy industrial automation names like Fanuc and ABB) with the higher-beta single-name SYM that more directly expresses the warehouse-robotics buildup phase the profile names as a next wave.

news_evidence.summary: BOTZ underperformed broad tech meaningfully in 2023 (+30% vs QQQ +54%) as legacy industrial robotics holdings lagged the AI/automation rally captured by single names.
Top BOTZ holdings (ABB, Fanuc, Keyence) reported soft 2024 capex outlooks from auto/manufacturing customers, drag on the ETF versus US-listed automation pure-plays.

removes.rationale: NuScale's flagship UAMPS Carbon Free Power Project was canceled in November 2023 due to cost overruns, a fundamental thesis breach for a pre-revenue SPAC whose only near-term commercial validation was that project; SMR exposure is better held through CCJ/CEG/BWXT/URA which have real cash flows or diversified miner exposure.

news_evidence.summary: NuScale and UAMPS cancel Carbon Free Power Project after target cost rose to $89/MWh; first US SMR deployment indefinitely delayed.
NuScale lays off 28% of workforce in January 2024 to extend cash runway after CFPP cancellation; no replacement customer announced.
Short-seller report flags NuScale revenue concentration in single Fluor-funded design contract; stock down ~60% from 2023 highs.

removes.rationale: The broad energy ETF dilutes the geopolitical-energy thesis with refiner/integrated weight; LNG (Cheniere) captures the Russia-Ukraine-driven European gas export tell more directly, and removing XLE frees a slot for the AI-power add VST without busting the 24-ticker cap.

news_evidence.summary: XLE flat YTD 2024 versus S&P 500 +10% as crude oil rangebound near $80 and integrateds lag on capex discipline concerns.
European gas (TTF) holds elevated levels into spring 2024 on continued Russian supply curtailment, supporting US LNG export economics; Cheniere reaffirms long-term contract pipeline.

rationale_overall: Two adds and three removes leave the watchlist at 23 of 24 tickers, sharpening exposure to two named next-wave themes while pruning weakest expressions. SYM replaces the broad BOTZ ETF with a higher-conviction warehouse-robotics single name riding Walmart and SoftBank/GreenBox commercial milestones. VST captures the AI-power leg of the nuclear thesis as a merchant nuclear operator levered to Texas data-center load growth, complementing CCJ/CEG/BWXT/URA. SMR exits on a hard fundamental thesis breach (UAMPS project canceled, layoffs, no replacement customer), and XLE exits as a redundant broad-energy holding when LNG already expresses the geopolitical gas tell more cleanly.
